title: 10 best herbal creams for mucus in throat

remedy_properties: Antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains compounds like echinacoside and chicoric acid, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, helping to alleviate mucus buildup.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in purple coneflower help to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.

Antioxidant activity: Purple coneflower's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect the respiratory tract cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and mucus production.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, making it easier to expel mucus. Additionally, Echinacea's ability to stimulate the production of white blood cells helps to fight off infections that can cause excessive mucus production, providing relief from a congested and sore throat.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce mucus production by inhibiting the activity of enzymes responsible for the production of mucopolysaccharides, which contribute to mucus formation.
Flavonoids (including Quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to expel mucus.
Alkylamides: These compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to boost the body's natural defenses against infections that cause mucus production, such as the common cold.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain a high concentration of echinacoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate throat congestion.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe a sore and congested throat.
Leaves: Contain volatile oils that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, providing relief from congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 cups of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the fresh echinacea flowers and leaves in a single layer in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers and leaves in 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the infused oil to a clean glass container.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by excess mucus.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as salicin and flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, allowing for easier clearing of mucus.

Demulcent properties: Slippery elm's demulcent properties create a protective film on the mucous membranes, trapping moisture and reducing dryness, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties. The cream soothes and protects the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The mucilage present in Ulmus rubra binds to the mucus, helping to loosen and clear it from the throat, providing quick relief from congestion and coughing. Regular use of Ulmus rubra creams may also help to prevent the formation of mucus in the throat, promoting a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages are complex, water-soluble polysaccharides that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with excess mucus.

Alkaloids (specifically, Ulmusine): Ulmusine, an alkaloid present in Ulmus rubra, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of a sore or irritated throat.

Triterpenoid saponins (specifically, Ursolic acid): Ursolic acid, a triterpenoid saponin found in Ulmus rubra, has been demonstrated to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, thereby alleviating mucus-related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Barks: Ulmus rubra bark is rich in tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that could help reduce mucus in the throat.
Leaves: Ulmus rubra leaves contain compounds like salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that could help soothe a sore throat and reduce mucus.
Buds: Ulmus rubra buds may contain similar compounds to the bark and leaves, which could help reduce inflammation and mucus in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ulmus rubra bark from mature trees during the fall season.
Combine the dried bark with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining 250ml of infused oil.
Mix 250ml of the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and reducing mucus production.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excess mucus.
Saponins: These compounds have expectorant properties, meaning they help to loosen and clear out thick or sticky mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and breathe.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The root extract of Glycyrrhiza glabra, commonly used in herbal creams, contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat. These properties enable the body to produce more mucus, which can then be easily cleared out, relieving coughs and congestion associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, it helps reduce inflammation and mucus production by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in the inflammatory response, thus soothing the throat.
Licoricidin: A phenolic glycoside, it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce mucus production and alleviate throat irritation, providing relief from cough and cold symptoms.
Liquiritigenin: A flavonoid, it exhibits mucolytic activity, breaking down mucus and making it easier to expel, thereby helping to clear the airways and alleviate respiratory congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to break down mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve mucus in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 500ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-80 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the oil.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of emulsifying wax to the oil mixture.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and reaches a consistency suitable for cream.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial infections in the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by excess mucus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort from excessive mucus production.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural decongestant properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that help break down and clear excess mucus. When applied topically as a cream, thyme's decongestant properties can help reduce swelling and congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. This natural remedy can provide relief from colds, flu, and sinus infections.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic compound that acts as an expectorant, helping to break down and clear mucus from the throat by thinning it and making it easier to cough up.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and clear mucus.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a phenolic compound that has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that can cause excessive mucus production in the throat, such as the common cold or flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly thymol, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Utilized for their ability to provide a sustained release of thymol and other essential oils, helping to soothe and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Buds: Employed for their concentrated essential oils, which contain thymol and other compounds that help to combat bacterial and viral infections in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves when in full bloom for maximum potency within 2 hours.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Infuse the mixture for 30 minutes over low heat while stirring occasionally to prevent scorching.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids for a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective gel-like substance called mucilage that coats and soothes the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and providing relief from irritated throats and coughs.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Expectorant properties: Marshmallow root has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and coughing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its soothing and protective properties. The root of the plant is rich in mucilages, a type of complex carbohydrate that forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes. When applied topically as a cream, these mucilages help to reduce inflammation and calm irritated tissues, making it easier to cough up mucus. This natural anti-inflammatory effect also helps to break up and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with excess mucus.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.

Triterpenoids (specifically, ursolic acid): Triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid, have expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, promoting a productive cough and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and moisturizes the throat.
Leaves: Similar to the roots, leaves of Althaea officinalis are also rich in mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and calm a sore throat.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain mucilage, which, when extracted, can be used to create creams and ointments that help to protect and moisturize the mucous membranes in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh althaea officinalis root and clean it thoroughly under cold running water for 3 minutes.
Combine the cleaned root with 100 ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth for 10 minutes.
Combine the strained liquid with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 40°c then whip it until it becomes a smooth and creamy texture for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Elderberry's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Elderberry has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with a sore or congested throat.
Antiviral Activity: Elderberry's antiviral properties can help combat viral infections that cause mucus buildup in the throat, such as the common cold or flu.

Expectorant Action: Elderflower, a key ingredient in many herbal elder creams, has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages and throat. Additionally, the antiviral properties of Sambucus nigra help to combat the underlying viral infections that cause excess mucus production. By soothing and protecting the mucous membranes, these creams provide quick relief from throat congestion and coughs. They also promote a healthy balance of mucus production.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to break down and thin mucus in the throat, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
Volatile oils (especially geraniol and linalool): These terpenes have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, reducing coughing and congestion.

Sambunigrin (an alkaloid glycoside): This compound has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, making it less conducive to mucus production.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams because of their high content of bioflavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce mucus and soothe the throat.
Leaves: They are used to make creams because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and relieve mucus buildup.
Fruits: They are used to make creams because they are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom.
Dry the sambucus nigra flowers and leaves using a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Steep 1 part of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in 5 parts of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 10% of the herbal extract with 90% of a natural cream base to create the final product.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the mucous membranes, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and reducing phlegm buildup.

Antioxidants and antimicrobial agents: Ginger's antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds help combat infections that can cause respiratory issues, such as bronchitis or pneumonia, by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting bacterial growth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties and natural expectorant abilities. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. This helps to relieve congestion and coughing associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Regular use of ginger creams may also help to soothe a sore throat and promote a healthy mucous membrane.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These are a type of phenolic compound found in ginger that help break down and clear mucus from the throat by reducing inflammation and acting as a natural expectorant.
Shogaols: These are another type of phenolic compound in ginger that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe an irritated throat and reduce mucus production.
Zingiberene: This is a sesquiterpene compound found in ginger that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections that can cause mucus buildup in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The part of the plant used for medicinal purposes, especially for digestive issues.
Buds: Buds: The young, unopened flower buds that contain essential oils and flavonoids, often used for their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale roots with clean tools and store them in a cool dry place for 2 days to allow any impurities to surface and be removed.
Grind 500 grams of the cleaned zingiber officinale roots in a blender or spice grinder to a fine powder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground zingiber officinale powder with 500 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while stirring frequently with a spatula until the beeswax dissolves completely.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and let it cool completely before use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, which can contribute to excess mucus production.
Expectorant Properties: Natural compounds in Ceylon cinnamon cream help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Soothes and calms irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a sore or congested throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, help to break down and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and coughs. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. This natural remedy is a popular choice for soothing a sore throat and relieving respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It helps to break down and thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the throat, and also has antimicrobial properties that can help combat underlying infections.

Cinnamic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow, and also has expectorant properties to help loosen mucus.

Eugenol: It has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat underlying infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow, and also has mucolytic properties to help break down and thin mucus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their expectorant properties to help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, providing relief from congestion.
Rhyzomes: Used for their warm, spicy, and antimicrobial properties to help ease congestion and reduce mucus production in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from sustainable sources for future use in the recipe.
Steam distill the cinnamomum verum bark to extract essential oils for 2 hours using a distillation apparatus.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler to create a base for the cream.
Combine 10g of the cinnamomum verum essential oil with 50g of shea butter and 20g of aloe vera gel.
Emulsify the mixture using a hand mixer for 5 minutes until a smooth and creamy texture is achieved.

remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint's natural expectorants help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the throat, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol in peppermint cream has anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling in the throat tissues and relieving discomfort and pain associated with mucus buildup.
Cough-suppressing properties: Peppermint's menthol also helps to suppress coughs by thinning out mucus, making it easier to expel, and providing temporary relief from coughing fits.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural decongestant properties. The active compound, menthol, creates a cooling sensation that helps to break down and clear excess mucus, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to thin out the mucus, reducing congestion and discomfort. This makes it a popular remedy for relieving symptoms of colds, sinusitis, and other respiratory issues. As a result, peppermint creams can provide quick and effective relief from a mucus-filled throat.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as an expectorant, thinning and loosening mucus in the throat, making it easier to cough up.
Catechins (Phenolic Compounds): Catechins exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, reducing the production of mucus and alleviating throat irritation.
Caryophyllene (Terpene): Caryophyllene has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce throat congestion and combat infections that may be contributing to mucus buildup.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita for creams to relieve mucus in the throat due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling sensation.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used for creams to help relieve mucus in the throat because they contain a high amount of volatile oils that help to thin mucus and promote expectoration.

Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are also used for creams to relieve mucus in the throat due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried mentha x piperita leaves in 500ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and mix it with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 50g of coconut oil to the mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Gradually add 100g of distilled water to the mixture while continuously stirring over low heat.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before pouring it into a container and refrigerating until set.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause excess mucus production, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm cream reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving discomfort associated with excessive mucus.
Expectorant properties: Lemon balm's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the throat, reducing congestion and coughing fits.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with mucus in throat because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, have been shown to reduce swelling and congestion in the throat. This helps to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, lemon balm's expectorant properties help to thin and remove excess mucus, providing quick relief from a sore and congested throat. This natural remedy is a popular choice for soothing a cough.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to clear mucus.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help thin and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up.
Limonene: This terpene has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce congestion and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and clear mucus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds such as melissic acid and rosmarinic acid, which possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Flowers: Their use is also prevalent due to the presence of volatile oils, which have a soothing effect on the throat and help to loosen mucus.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are occasionally used, likely due to their ability to contribute to the overall extract's efficacy and shelf life when combined with the leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them completely in a warm place.
Combine 30g of dried melissa officinalis with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to the mixture and heat for 10 more minutes in the double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with mucus in throat because its active ingredient, eucalyptol, possesses natural decongestant properties that effectively break down and eliminate excess mucus. When applied topically to the throat, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer airway. Additionally, the menthol present in the cream helps to thin out thick mucus, making it easier to expel, providing fast and effective relief from congestion and coughs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The creams contain eucalyptol, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the mucous membranes, making it easier to clear out mucus.
Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains compounds like cineole, which helps thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and expel from the body.

Antiviral and antibacterial activity: The creams' antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that cause excessive mucus production, such as colds and flu, by inhibiting the growth of viruses and bacteria.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate coughing due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Barks: Containing tannins, which aid in soothing irritated mucous membranes and reducing the thickness of mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and wash them thoroughly with filtered water.
Dry the leaves in shade for 7-10 days or until crispy and store them in airtight containers.
Mix one part dried eucalyptus leaves with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and filter paper to remove solids, then add 5% beeswax and stir until melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that work synergistically to soothe and clear out excess mucus. The extracts from the maidenhair tree, specifically its leaves, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the cream's emollient texture provides long-lasting hydration, helping to loosen and break down stubborn mucus, allowing for a more effective clearance of the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe mucous membranes.
Barks: Contains alkaloids and tannins that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing throat congestion.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves using ethanol solvent in a ratio of 1:2 for 2 hours at room temperature.
Mix the extracted liquid with coconut oil and beeswax in a ratio of 3:1:1 and heat until melted.
Add 10% of vitamin e oil to enhance moisturizing properties and reduce inflammation.
Pour the mixture into molds and refrigerate for 30 minutes to set before packaging.
Allow the cream to mature at room temperature for 24 hours before use to allow flavors to develop.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Adiantum spp. has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe.
Antioxidant: The plant's antioxidant compounds can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate respiratory issues like chronic bronchitis or asthma.
Mucolytic: Adiantum spp. has been traditionally used to break down mucus and reduce its thickness, making it easier to cough up and expel from the lungs.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with mucus in throat because of its unique properties that target the root cause of congestion. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in turmeric, such as curcumin, reduce swelling and irritation in the mucous membranes, allowing for smoother airflow. Additionally, the antioxidants in turmeric help to break down and clear out excess mucus, relieving discomfort and promoting a healthy respiratory system. As a result, using an herbal turmeric cream can provide rapid relief from throat congestion and coughs, helping to restore normal breathing and vocal function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds (curcuminoids): Help reduce inflammation in the throat, which can alleviate discomfort and congestion caused by excess mucus.
Antimicrobial properties: Inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that may be contributing to a sore or infected throat, allowing for faster recovery.
Mucolytic activity (pectin): Break down excess mucus into smaller, easier-to-clear particles, making it less sticky and more manageable for the body to expel.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat respiratory issues, including mucus buildup in the throat, due to their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Crush 3 tablespoons of dried turmeric root into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of grated beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of crushed curcuma longa powder to the melted wax mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain a unique combination of active compounds that have natural expectorant properties. The mucilage present in fenugreek binds to excess mucus, helping to loosen and clear it from the throat and airways, relieving congestion and coughs. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of fenugreek cream reduce swelling and discomfort associated with a sore or irritated throat, promoting a more comfortable respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fenugreek's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughs.
Anti-inflammatory property: Fenugreek's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, helping to soothe a sore or irritated throat caused by excessive mucus production.
Antioxidant property: Fenugreek's antioxidant properties help protect the mucous membranes in the throat from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy tissue function and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated throat tissues.
Seeds: The seeds contain saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort in the throat.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of fenugreek contain compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat.
remedy_recipe: Dry and powder 100g of trigonella foenum graecum leaves and stems.
Mix 20g of the powdered herb with 40g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 30g of sweet almond oil and 10g of coconut oil to the mixture and stir until combined.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and let cool before transferring the cream to a glass jar and storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain a high concentration of allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated mucous membranes. The cream's gentle, non-irritating properties allow it to penetrate deep into the throat, breaking down excess mucus and reducing its viscosity, making it easier to expel. Additionally, comfrey's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties help to combat underlying infections that can cause excessive mucus production, providing long-lasting relief from a stubborn cough and congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow, and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in comfrey cream neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the mucous membranes, helping to soothe and protect the throat from irritation.

Soothing and demulcent properties: Comfrey's soothing and demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the surface of the skin, providing relief from itchiness, dryness, and irritation caused by excessive mucus production in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, comfrey roots help soothe and calm irritated throats, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes aid in reducing swelling and congestion, promoting a healthier throat environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and stems, allowing them to dry completely before use.
Grind 20g of dried symphytum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground symphytum officinale with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 65°c.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken, then pour it into sterilized containers and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, have been shown to reduce mucus production and thin out existing mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the creams' antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that can cause excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and coughing associated with a sore throat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to clear out excess mucus.

Expectorant properties: Dandelion's natural expectorants, like inulin and sesquiterpenes, can help loosen and break down thick mucus, allowing for easier coughing up of congestion and relieving a sore or clogged throat.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's high antioxidant content helps protect the throat from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive mucus production, which can exacerbate inflammation and discomfort in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a natural expectorant that helps to thin and clear mucus from the throat.
Fruits: Contain mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers at dawn when they are most potent containing taraxasterol.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried dandelion flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 1/4 cup of cold-pressed coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with mucus in throat because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues. The cream's active ingredients, such as calendula, work to break down and clear out excess mucus, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of pot marigold creams help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the throat, making it easier to swallow and speak without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, which can contribute to the formation of excess mucus.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as luteolin and apigenin, can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing swelling and congestion.
Expectorant properties: Pot marigold's expectorant action can help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the throat, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at soothing irritated mucous membranes in the throat.
Seeds: The seeds of the pot marigold plant are rich in essential oils that have natural expectorant properties, helping to break down and clear mucus from the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the calendula flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 24 hours then store them airtight.
Mix one cup of dried calendula flowers with two cups of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for seven to ten days shaking occasionally then strain through cheesecloth.
Blend the infused oil with beeswax and sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2:1 and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The herb plantain has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it an effective remedy for relieving a sore or scratchy throat. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it directly targets the affected area, providing instant relief from mucus buildup and discomfort. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain creams help reduce inflammation in the throat, which can alleviate discomfort and swelling caused by excess mucus.
Antimicrobial properties: Plantain's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections, reducing the production of mucus and promoting healing.
Expectorant properties: The natural expectorants in plantain creams help loosen and clear out mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, making them effective for relieving congestion and coughs.

Barks: The barks of the plantain plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma, due to their antispasmodic properties, which help reduce inflammation and relax airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry plantago major leaves for three weeks under shade or low humidity conditions.
Combine one part dried plantago major leaves with two parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four weeks, shaking daily, to allow infusion of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids, and add 1% vitamin e oil as an emollient.
Mix well and fill sterilized tubes or jars, allowing the cream to mature at room temperature for two weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain the potent anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow herb. The cream's active ingredients, including azulene and chamazulene, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat, allowing for easier breathing and a reduction in excess mucus production. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties help to break down and clear out thick mucus, providing instant relief from a sore and congested throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause excessive mucus production in the throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, which can alleviate congestion and coughing associated with excess mucus.
Expectorant properties: Yarrow's saponins and terpenes help break down and loosen thickened mucus in the throat, making it easier to expel and reducing the risk of further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help break down and remove excess mucus from the respiratory tract.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers weighing approximately one ounce.
Combine the dried herb with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of two parts oil to one part herb.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the herbal properties into the oil.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 24 hours.
Whip the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of one part wax to two parts oil until creamy consistency is achieved.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for mucus in throat are topical ointments infused with natural herbs that help to break down and clear excess mucus from the throat, providing quick and effective relief from congestion and discomfort. The best herbal creams for mucus in throat include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Salvia officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Hydrastis canadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Sambucus nigra. If left untreated, this condition can lead to a persistent cough, difficulty swallowing, and disrupted sleep patterns, ultimately affecting daily productivity, work performance, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to mucus in throat that are treated with herbal creams are sore throats, tonsillitis, and pharyngitis. These herbal creams typically contain ingredients like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and chamomile, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. They help to reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote healing by creating a protective barrier against irritants and promoting the natural drainage of mucus.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with mucus in throat is a blend of Echinacea, Thyme, and Slippery Elm. Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, while Thyme's expectorant properties loosen and clear mucus. Slippery Elm soothes and protects the mucous membranes, promoting a healthy environment for healing. This combination can be applied topically as a cream, or used as a warm compress to help alleviate throat congestion and promote a speedy recovery. Regular use can help prevent recurring mucus buildup.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), herbal creams like Throat Coat, which is a traditional demulcent herbal tea, have been found to be effective in providing relief from mucus in the throat. The study involved 60 patients with acute pharyngitis, who were randomly assigned to receive either Throat Coat or a placebo. The results showed that Throat Coat significantly reduced the intensity of throat pain when swallowing, with a reduction of 43.8 points in the sum of pain intensity differences (SPID) compared to 16.5 points in the placebo group.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with mucus in throat because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, work to break down and clear mucus, relieving congestion and coughs. This natural remedy also has a soothing effect on the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. The cooling sensation of Eucalyptus globulus creams helps to temporarily numb the throat, providing quick relief from mucus and congestion, making it a popular treatment for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: Acts as a bronchodilator and decongestant by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel, thus providing relief from a congested throat.
Cineole: Exhibits expectorant properties by stimulating the production of mucus and increasing its flow, making it easier to cough up mucus and reduce throat congestion.
α-Pinene: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections and exacerbate mucus production in the throat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps to break down mucus and relieve congestion.

Barks: Eucalyptus bark is used because it contains a higher concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making it effective in relieving throat congestion.

Fruits: Eucalyptus fruits are used, although less commonly, due to their rich content of eucalyptol and other compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote the expulsion of mucus in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 15-20 fresh leaves of eucalyptus globulus in the morning when the dew is still present on them.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to prevent any fungal growth.
Combine 250ml of coconut oil with 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler to melt them together slowly.
Add 20-25 dried eucalyptus globulus leaves to the melted mixture and steep for 2-3 hours to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and bottle it in a dark glass container to preserve its potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with mucus in throat because of its potent anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, Echinacea's antimicrobial properties help fight off underlying infections that can cause excessive mucus production, providing relief from congestion and coughing. This natural remedy can help soothe and protect the throat, promoting a faster recovery from respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with excessive mucus production in the throat.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation, leading to a decrease in mucus production and congestion.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives: These phenolic compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating symptoms of excessive mucus production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used in creams for mucus in the throat because they contain a high concentration of echinacoside, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea purpurea are used in creams for mucus in the throat because they contain a high concentration of alkylamides, which have been shown to have immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory effects.

Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are used in creams for mucus in the throat because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of echinacea purpurea flowers at the peak of flowering season in july and august.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 1 cup of organic coconut oil at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Strain and discard the solids then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the infused oil.
Whip the mixture at room temperature for 10 minutes until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Glycyrrhizin helps break down and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of Glycyrrhiza glabra can help reduce throat irritation and discomfort, providing relief from coughs and congestion. This makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating mucus-related throat issues.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps reduce inflammation and mucus production in the throat by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to mucus buildup.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and mucus production.

Triterpenes (specifically 18α-glycyrrhetinic acid): They help reduce inflammation and mucus production by inhibiting the action of pro-inflammatory enzymes and modulating the immune response, which in turn helps to reduce mucus buildup in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes.
Leaves: They are used to make a tea that helps to relieve coughs and soothe the throat due to their expectorant properties.
Seeds: They are used in creams to help relieve coughs and reduce mucus production due to their anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties.
remedy_recipe: Steep 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 100 ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal extract.
Mix 20 grams of distilled water with 10 grams of the herbal extract in a clean container.
Add 5 grams of vegetable glycerin and 2 grams of beeswax to the mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes to create a smooth and consistent cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural decongestant properties. Ginger has been traditionally used to relieve respiratory issues, such as congestion and coughs, due to its ability to break down and clear mucus. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing quick relief from coughs and sore throats. Regular application of the cream can help to alleviate symptoms and promote a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat, making it easier to clear mucus.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with mucus buildup in the throat.
Terpenes (specifically, sesquiterpenes): Terpenes in ginger have expectorant properties, meaning they help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe freely.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve mucus congestion.
Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to produce essential oils, which are then extracted and used in creams to help thin out mucus and soothe the throat.

Barks: The barks of the Zingiber officinale plant are used to make creams for mucus in the throat because they contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce swelling and combat infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of zingiber officinale rhizomes, 100g of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax.
Grate 50g of zingiber officinale rhizomes and mix with 50g of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the zingiber officinale is fragrant.
Add 100g of beeswax to the mixture and stir until the beeswax has melted.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain the essential oils of thyme, which have natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol in thyme oil helps to break down and clear out excess mucus, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, providing relief from sore throats and coughs. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated throats.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps to break down and reduce mucus in the throat by its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which also aid in soothing irritated mucous membranes.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus from the throat, thereby relieving congestion and coughs.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and alleviate symptoms of mucus buildup, such as coughing and soreness, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their essential oils, particularly thymol, possess antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and reduce mucus production.
Stems: Like leaves, stems contain thymol, which contributes to their decongestant and antimicrobial properties in throat creams.
Flowers: The flowers of Thymus vulgaris contain thymol and other essential oils, which help to soothe the throat, reduce inflammation, and thin out mucus.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried thyme flowers in 500ml of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate the oil.
Combine 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the beeswax.
Add 100ml of the thyme infused oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the throat. The essential oils present in Salvia officinalis, such as thujone and borneol, have decongestant properties that help break down and clear mucus, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, the herb's expectorant properties help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, providing quick relief from throat irritation and congestion. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate throat issues.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and suppress the production of mucus in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow.
Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help to break down and clear out excess mucus in the throat, relieving congestion and coughing.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has expectorant properties that help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Salvia officinalis are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to soothe and calm the throat.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used, as they contain some of the same essential oils as the leaves, which can help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Infuse the dried leaves in 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit.
Add the infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture and stir until the cream thickens and cools to around 120 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain peppermint oil, which has natural decongestant properties. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to thin and break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel. This can provide quick relief from congestion and coughs associated with a sore throat. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of mentha piperita may help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to thin and break down mucus, making it easier to expel from the throat, and also has a cooling effect that can provide relief from throat irritation.

Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the throat, which can contribute to the formation of excess mucus, and helps to open up airways for easier breathing.

Rosmarinic acid: Acts as an antitussive, helping to suppress coughing and reduce the amount of mucus produced in the throat, while also exhibiting antioxidant properties to protect the throat from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have decongestant properties.
Stems: The stems are used for their menthol content, which helps to thin mucus and provide relief from congestion.
Roots: The roots contain a smaller amount of menthol than the leaves and stems but are still used to make creams due to their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves at peak potency in early morning.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Mix 10% essential oil with 90% coconut oil in a glass container with a spoon.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and heat in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis creams helps with mucus in throat because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's key compound, berberine, has been shown to break down and expel mucus, reducing congestion and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing effects calm the mucous membranes, allowing them to heal and recover from irritation. As a natural expectorant, Hydrastis canadensis creams provide relief from coughs and sore throats, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues and throat health.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: They help break down and reduce mucus production in the throat by inhibiting the activity of enzymes responsible for mucus production.
Phenolics: They have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation in the throat, making it easier to clear mucus.
Tannins: They have astringent properties that help dry up and reduce excess mucus in the throat, providing temporary relief from congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis are often used in creams for mucus in the throat due to their high content of berberine, which has antimicrobial properties that help reduce mucus production.
Leaves: The leaves are utilized in creams because they are rich in hydrastine, a compound that exhibits expectorant properties, aiding in the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of Hydrastis canadensis are used in creams due to their ability to reduce inflammation and combat infection in the throat, thus alleviating mucus buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis roots in the fall season when mature.
Extract the active principles of the roots using 500ml of 70% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Strain the ethanol extract and discard the solids then mix it with 500ml of distilled water.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the hydrastis canadensis extract mixture to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and blend until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with mucus in throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The essential oils present in these creams, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the throat, making it easier to expel mucus. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of lavender oil can help to reduce coughing and promote a sense of relaxation, allowing the body to recover more efficiently from respiratory issues. This makes Lavandula angustifolia creams a popular natural remedy for soothing a sore and congested throat.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to reduce mucus in the throat by acting as an expectorant, making it easier to cough up excess mucus.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and clear mucus.
Rosmarinic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and reduce inflammation that can contribute to mucus buildup.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the throat.
Leaves: Lavender leaves are used for their calming effects and their ability to reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for throat creams.
Stems: Lavender stems are used for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections and reduce mucus production in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried flowers at peak bloom for maximum oil content and potency in 2 hours.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusion in 2 weeks.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids in 30 minutes.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50ml of the infused oil in a double boiler for emulsification in 20 minutes.
Add a few drops of vitamin e oil and pour the mixture into a clean container for packaging in 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with mucus in throat because they contain extracts from the elderberry plant, which is rich in antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as sambucol and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat. This helps to break down excess mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear the airways. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties help to calm an irritated throat, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a smoother recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Phenolic acids (including salicylic acid): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with mucus buildup in the throat.
Volatile oils (including limonene and pinene): These organic compounds have expectorant properties, helping to break down and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Sambucus nigra flowers are primarily used to make creams for mucus in the throat due to their high content of antitussive compounds, particularly saponins and flavonoids.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for mucus in the throat due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes.

Fruits: The fruits of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for mucus in the throat due to their high content of anthocyanins and other antioxidants, which have been shown to have antitussive and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 sambucus nigra flowers, leaves and stems by hand when in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Extract the dried plant material using a carrier oil such as olive or coconut oil in a 1:3 ratio.
Combine the extracted oil with 10-20% beeswax in a double boiler to create a smooth creamy texture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage.
